question_text: A rectangular block exerts a force of 120 N on a table. When resting on its smallest face (0.2 m × 0.3 m), the pressure is 2000 Pa. When the same block is placed on its largest face, the pressure becomes 1000 Pa. What is the area of the largest face?
explanation: Using pressure = force/area: for the smallest face, area = 120/2000 = 0.06 m² (which checks: 0.2 × 0.3 = 0.06 m²). For the largest face, 1000 = 120/area, so area = 120/1000 = 0.12 m².
